question_title: What will be the value of "CBOE S&P 500 3-Month Volatility Index" on 2026-02-26?
summary: CBOE S&P 500 3-Month Volatility Index (VXVCLS) forecast for Feb 26, 2026. Starting from 21.60 on Feb 17, with 7 trading days to resolution. Two-model Monte Carlo approach: empirical bootstrap (captures fat tails) and Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reverting model (captures structural mean reversion toward ~19.68 with 4.5-day half-life). Blended 60/40 in favor of bootstrap. Current elevated vol regime (AI disruption, tariffs, Nasdaq weakness) supports above-average levels, but mean reversion pulls median below the starting point. Distribution is right-skewed, reflecting VIX-type spike risk.

factors.description: Current VIX3M level at 21.60 (Feb 17) serves as strong anchor - 7 trading days is short horizon where starting value dominates
OU mean-reversion model: long-run mean ~19.68, half-life 4.5 days - strong pull downward from current elevated level of 21.60
Elevated volatility regime: AI disruption, software selloff, Nasdaq 5-week losing streak, tariff/trade policy uncertainty
Upcoming catalysts: FOMC minutes (Feb 18), PCE inflation data - could trigger volatility moves in either direction
VIX-type right-skew: spikes are more extreme than declines, fattening the upper tail of the distribution

reasoning:

The key considerations:

- **Current value**: VIX3M at 21.60 (Feb 17), FRED VXVCLS at 22.17 (Feb 13)
- **Mean reversion**: OU model estimates long-run mean ~19.68 with 4.5-day half-life, pulling values down
- **Elevated regime**: AI disruption, software selloff, Nasdaq 5-week losing streak, tariff uncertainty support continued above-average volatility
- **Catalysts ahead**: FOMC minutes, PCE inflation data could push volatility either direction
- **Right-skew**: VIX-type indices have fat right tails (spikes are larger than drops)
- **Monte Carlo results**: Blended (60% bootstrap + 40% OU) from 21.60 over 7 trading days**Forecast Summary for VXVCLS on 2026-02-26:**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 18.7 |
| 20th | 19.5 |
| 40th | 20.5 |
| 60th | 21.5 |
| 80th | 22.8 |
| 90th | 24.0 |

**Median estimate: ~21.0** (between 40th and 60th percentiles)

Key drivers: Starting from 21.60 with 7 trading days, the distribution reflects tension between mean-reversion pull toward ~19.7 and the current elevated volatility regime. The right-skewed shape reflects VIX-type spike risk from catalysts like FOMC minutes and PCE data.
